description: The `/coding` slash-command expansion and auto-complete work is tracked as its own harness task so future sessions can resume from the new command grammar, index selection, and harness-completion semantics without rediscovering them live.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it only tracks the `/coding` slash-command and auto-complete harness.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify the reboot entry records the current baseline: `/coding status` already exists, `running` pushes are disabled, and completed harness creation already skips confirmation.
Confirm the scope remains limited to Telegram `/coding` orchestration, task lifecycle, and status reporting rather than changing the Codex worker model or target-repo harness file format.

description: Telegram `/coding` recognizes explicit slash subcommands for list, status, pause, resume, and stop without regressing the existing `/coding <repo> <goal>` start flow.
steps: Send `/coding list`, `/coding status`, `/coding pause`, `/coding resume`, and `/coding stop` and verify each routes to the correct control path instead of the start-command parser.
Send `/coding <repo> <goal>` and verify it still creates and launches a coding task.
Verify malformed or unsupported slash subcommands return a clear coding-task usage error instead of creating a task.

description: Telegram `/coding list` shows the current private-chat task list with 1-based indexes, and indexed slash commands resolve against that list newest-first.
steps: Create multiple coding tasks for the same Telegram private chat and verify `/coding list` shows them newest-first with index, task id, status, repo, goal summary, and recoverability.
Run `/coding status 2`, `/coding pause 2`, `/coding resume 2`, and `/coding stop 2` and verify each targets the second listed task.
Verify non-numeric or out-of-range indexes return a clear error without mutating any task.

description: Pause and resume use recoverable lifecycle semantics while slash stop ends the selected task as a terminal state.
steps: Pause a running task and verify the worker is interrupted and the task becomes `waiting_user` so it can be resumed later.
Resume a paused or failed task and verify the launcher relaunches it back into `starting`.
Stop a task and verify it becomes terminal (`cancelled`) rather than merely recoverable.

description: When the target repo harness reports all work complete, nanobot automatically marks the coding task as completed without requiring any user confirmation.
steps: Prepare a repo whose `PLAN.json` has all `passes=true`, refresh a task in `starting`, `running`, or `waiting_user`, and verify nanobot transitions it to `completed` automatically.
Verify the auto-complete path does not require a Telegram confirmation message or an extra control command from the user.
Verify unfinished harnesses do not trigger the completed transition.

description: Telegram proactive pushes stay lifecycle-only: starting, waiting_user, completed, and failed still notify, while running progress remains query-only behind status commands.
steps: Trigger a starting task and verify Telegram sends one start notification.
Advance a task to `running` and verify poll-loop progress refreshes do not emit proactive Telegram messages.
Verify `waiting_user`, `completed`, and `failed` continue to send their dedicated lifecycle notifications.

description: Focused tests cover slash-command parsing, list/index selection, lifecycle transitions, auto-complete behavior, and status reporting without regressing the current coding-task routing.
steps: Run focused pytest for routing, policy or selection behavior, progress monitoring, notifier behavior, and CLI status coverage.
Verify the suite covers `/coding list`, indexed slash commands, slash stop terminal semantics, and automatic completion when the repo harness is finished.
Confirm `.venv/bin/python -m compileall` still succeeds for the touched coding-task modules and tests.

description: README and PROGRESS.md document the new `/coding` subcommands, index-based targeting, and automatic completion semantics for finished harnesses.
steps: Open README.md and verify it documents `/coding list`, indexed status or control commands, and the lifecycle-only Telegram push policy.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify it records the baseline, the new slash-command decisions, and the auto-complete behavior for finished repo harnesses.
Confirm PLAN.json is fully marked complete after implementation and verification pass.
